steps.text: A wild CONTRACTOR appeared on the jobsite!
"We're here in a spirit of partnering and collaboration."
CONTRACTOR slides a submittal package across the table...
The QC stamp is from a company that no longer exists.
Choose your response, ENGINEER.
Both parties attend the preconstruction partnering session.
Everyone pledges to communicate openly and resolve issues at the lowest level.
They sign a partnering charter.
By the second progress meeting, every email is CC'd to supervisors three levels up.
The partnering charter yellows on the trailer wall. The battle begins.
CONTRACTOR has installed material before the submittal was approved.
The Government arrives on-site. The stamp says 'Revise and Resubmit.'
"The lead time was 12 weeks. We had no choice."
"That's a you problem. Remove and replace."
It's on.
CONTRACTOR declares substantial completion.
The Government arrives with eight inspectors, flashlights, and moisture meters.
And decades of accumulated grievances.
The resulting punch list will run to 47 pages. Retainage stays frozen.
The final battle begins.
